etymology.description: 〇 representing top edge of 鼎 (dǐng, "ding") -> roundness -> member
etymology.interpretation: 〇 indicates the top edge of a 鼎 (dǐng, "ding") representing roundness, which metaphorically connects to the idea of a group of members forming a unified whole.
etymology.simplification: 員(員): 貝(貝) (bèi, "cowry") was simplified to 贝(貝) in the character 員 to form the simplified character 员, which visually reduces the strokes.

examples.chinese: 他是一名团队成员(他是一名團隊成員)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shì yī míng tuánduì chéngyuán.
examples.english: He is a member of the team.

examples.chinese: 公司需要招募新员工(公司需要招募新員工)。
examples.english: The company needs to recruit new members of staff.

examples.chinese: 俱乐部的所有成员都喜欢运动(俱樂部的所有成員都喜歡運動)。
examples.pinyin: Jùlèbù de suǒyǒu chéngyuán dōu xǐhuān yùndòng.
examples.english: All the members of the club like sports.
